title: AI Roadmap for Network Engineers
summary: A structured roadmap outlining the journey from networking fundamentals to AI-driven intelligent network operations.
outcome: Created a clear learning path for network engineers transitioning into AI and intelligent networking.
content:
This project defines a structured career path for network engineers transitioning into AI-driven networking.

Stage 1: Networking Foundation — Build strong expertise in TCP/IP, routing, switching, BGP, MPLS, SD-WAN, and network security.

Stage 2: Automation — Learn Python and tools such as APIs, Netmiko, NAPALM, and Ansible.

Stage 3: Data — Work with telemetry, logs, and time-series data.

Stage 4: AI & Machine Learning — Apply ML for anomaly detection and traffic prediction.

Stage 5: Intelligent Networks — Build self-healing and AI-driven networks.

This roadmap bridges traditional networking with AI-driven operations.

title: Understanding Python Projects in 3 Simple Layers
summary: A beginner-friendly explanation of how Python projects work using a house-building analogy: environment, package manager, and libraries.
content:
When I started learning Python for network automation, I realized that running a Python project is not just about writing code. There is a structure behind it.

🏗️ Think of a Python project like building your own house

Environment (venv) → Your land / workspace
It keeps your project isolated and avoids conflicts with other projects.
Commands:
python3 -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate

Package Manager (pip) → Your tool store
Installs and manages libraries. You go there to download what your project needs.
Command:
pip install --upgrade pip

Libraries (pyyaml, netmiko) → Your tools & materials
Each library solves a specific problem.
- pyyaml → Read YAML files
- netmiko → Network automation
Command:
pip install pyyaml

🧠 No land → nowhere to build
🧰 No tool store → nothing gets done
🧱 No tools/materials → no functionality

🚀 Master these 3 layers and you can build any Python project confidently, especially in network automation.
